QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_109

question.text: A speculator is bullish on TCS. TCS spot is Rs. 3,500 and futures are at Rs. 3,530. He has two choices: buy 1,000 shares in cash (Rs. 35 lakhs) or buy 5 lots of futures (lot size 200, margin 15%). If TCS rises 5%, compare the return on investment for both strategies.

answer.text: Cash market: 5% return on Rs. 35 lakhs = Rs. 1,75,000 (5% ROI). Futures: same Rs. 1,75,000 profit but on margin of Rs. 5,29,500 = approximately 33% ROI. Futures deliver higher ROI due to leverage
Both strategies give 5% return because the underlying price change is the same
Cash market gives higher return because there is no cost of carry
Futures give lower return because the entry price (3,530) is higher than spot (3,500)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Step-by-step calculation: Cash market: - Investment = 1,000 x 3,500 = Rs. 35,00,000 - Profit if 5% rise = 35,00,000 x 5% = Rs. 1,75,000 - ROI = 1,75,000 / 35,00,000 = 5%
Futures market: - Exposure = 5 lots x 200 x 3,530 = Rs. 35,30,000 - Profit = 1,000 shares x 3,500 x 5% = Rs. 1,75,000 (approximately) - Margin = 35,30,000 x 15% = Rs. 5,29,500 - ROI = 1,75,000 / 5,29,500 = approximately 33%
**The Trap:** The return on INVESTED CAPITAL is 33% for futures vs 5% for cash. The absolute profit is similar, but leverage amplifies the ROI.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Leverage advantage (and risk) of futures over cash market is a key Series VIII concept.
**Key Takeaway:** Futures leverage amplifies ROI: 5% market move = 33% return on margin (with 15% margin requirement).
